saanya_sreenivas.name: Saanya Sreenivas
saanya_sreenivas.summary:
Representing California, Saanya Sreenivas knows the feeling of standing atop the podium, currently competing at the U15 level, a moment that separates contenders from champions. With 23 entries across 9 tournaments, the competitive foundation is built — and more titles are within reach.

Saanya is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 1 singles title, a doubles best of #2, a mixed doubles best of #4. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries).

With 9 different doubles partners over the years, Saanya adapts to anyone:
  • Samyukta Karthik (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in GD (4 events, best: #2, across 2 seasons)

Saanya has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With tournaments spanning NE, NW, NorCal, Saanya isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Saanya brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

saatvik_b_sunilraj.name: Saatvik B. Sunilraj
saatvik_b_sunilraj.summary:
Out of Virginia, Saatvik B. Sunilraj has collected 3 tournament titles, currently competing at the U19 level, capturing 3 titles across 19 tournaments and 53 entries. Each win is earned through bracket battles against hungry opponents — and Saatvik keeps finding ways to come out on top.

Notably, Saatvik has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Saatvik especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 doubles title, 2 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (6 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries).

Saatvik has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 16 different players across the career:
  • Kevin Zhao — a trusted BD/U17 partner (6 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Anvitha B Ranganna — a trusted U17/XD partner (6 events, best: #5, across 2 seasons)
  • Yoning Liu — 3 events in XD, best: #2, across 2 seasons

Saatvik has been on this journey through U15, U17, U19 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Saatvik has competed across 4 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 8 seeded entries across the career, Saatvik consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 5 seasons and 3 titles, Saatvik has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

saatvik_shetty.name: Saatvik Shetty
saatvik_shetty.summary:
Representing California, Saatvik Shetty is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-3 finish at Selection places Saatvik in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Saatvik is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (5 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Saatvik is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 3 singles titles, 5 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-3 (U17 BD) • Junior Nationals champion (BS U17) • 7 ORC titles.

With 14 different doubles partners over the years, Saatvik adapts to anyone:
  • Vincent Chen (current primary partner) — 5 events together this season alone with 3 titles, building serious momentum (5 total events in BD/U17, across 2 seasons)
  • Annie Meng (current partner) — 2 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Terence Cai (current partner) — currently teaming up in BD (3 events, best: #5, across 2 seasons)
  • Jennifer Bangcaya — a trusted XD partner (4 events, best: #5, across 2 seasons)

The 2025-2026 season has been a step up, with 4 titles across 3 tournaments — a clear sign that Saatvik is still ascending.

Saatvik has been on this journey through U13, U15, U17, U19 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Saatvik has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Saatvik has been seeded #1 or #2 in 5 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 9 titles, Saatvik is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

Hailing from Georgia, Sai Ratan Shankar has reached the podium at Junior Nationals, finishing top-3 in BS U11, BD U11. At the biggest event on the calendar, making the final four is a statement: Sai belongs with the best in the country.

What makes Sai especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #2, 2 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals top-3 (BS U11, BD U11) • 2 ORC titles.

Sai has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 10 different players across the career:
  • Aditya Namboodiripad (current primary partner) — 11 events together this season alone with 2 titles, building serious momentum (11 total events in BD, across 2 seasons)
  • Sophia Liu (current primary partner) — 5 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (5 total events in XD, across 2 seasons)

Sai has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Sai has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 14 seeded entries across the career, Sai consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Sai brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

Notably, Sai has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Sai competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, a doubles best of #2, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Sai has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 8 different players across the career:
  • Mehan Murthysundar (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (3 events, best: #2)

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 5 podium finishes across 6 tournaments. Sai is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Sai has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Sai's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

Selena is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Selena is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #2, 2 doubles titles, 4 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-3 (U15 XD, U15 GD) • Junior Nationals top-2 (XD U15, GD U15) • 3 ORC titles.

Selena has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 22 different players across the career:
  • Terence Cai (current primary partner) — 3 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (3 total events in XD)
  • Katie Wong (current partner) — currently teaming up in GD (2 events, best: #3)

The 2025-2026 season tells a story of acceleration, with 1 title across 4 tournaments — a clear sign that Selena is still ascending.

Selena has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Selena has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 6 seeded entries across the career, Selena consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 5 seasons and 6 titles, Selena has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

shaurya_agrawal.name: Shaurya Agrawal
shaurya_agrawal.summary:
A Illinois competitor, Shaurya Agrawal is on an upward trajectory, currently competing at the U19 level, with 11 entries across 5 tournaments. Every tournament adds experience, every match sharpens the game.

Notably, Shaurya has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Shaurya especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

Shaurya has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 5 different players across the career:
  • Rasika Gokhale (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (3 events, best: #5)

Shaurya has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Shaurya's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

shaurya_batra.name: Shaurya Batra
shaurya_batra.summary:
Out of Virginia, Shaurya Batra has shown real competitive teeth, currently competing at the U13 level, breaking into the semifinals 2 times. Across 26 entries in 9 tournaments, every deep run is a signal of growing strength.

A true triple-threat, Shaurya competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

With 6 different doubles partners over the years, Shaurya adapts to anyone:
  • Shriya Batra (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (8 events, spanning 3 seasons)

Shaurya has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Shaurya brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

What makes Shaurya especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

shaurya_maddela.name: Shaurya Maddela
shaurya_maddela.summary:
Out of Texas, Shaurya Maddela has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-4), currently competing at the U15 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Shaurya's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

Shaurya is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 1 singles title, 1 doubles title, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (4 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (6 entries) • ORC top-4 finisher.

Shaurya has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 26 different players across the career:
  • Varish Karri (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in BD (5 events, best: #3)
  • Yeewei Yap — a trusted BD partner (6 events, best: #2, across 2 seasons)

A veteran of the junior circuit, Shaurya has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Shaurya has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seeded entries across the career, Shaurya consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 5 seasons and 2 titles, Shaurya has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

shawn_xia.name: Shawn Xia
shawn_xia.summary:
Representing New Jersey, Shawn Xia has reached the podium at Junior Nationals, finishing top-3 in BD U19. At the biggest event on the calendar, making the final four is a statement: Shawn belongs with the best in the country.

What makes Shawn especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #2, 2 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (8 entries) • Junior Nationals top-3 (BD U19) • 1 ORC title.

Shawn has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 10 different players across the career:
  • Chris Wu (current primary partner) — 6 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (15 total events in BD/U17, spanning 5 seasons)
  • Kavya Krishna (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (4 events, best: #4, spanning 3 seasons)
  • Mikayla Cruz — 2 events, 1 title together in XD

Shawn has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Shawn has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 3 titles, Shawn is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

shraddha_prakash.name: Shraddha Prakash
shraddha_prakash.summary:
Representing Texas, Shraddha Prakash has climbed to the top of the mountain at Junior Nationals, the crown jewel of the American junior badminton season in GS U15, GD U15. That title represents the culmination of grueling competition against the very best in the country. With 21 career victories across 25 tournaments, Shraddha has proven the ability to deliver when it matters most.

Shraddha is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

What makes Shraddha especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting 9 singles titles, 7 doubles titles, 5 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals champion (GS U15, GD U15) • 8 ORC titles.

Diving into the match data reveals: Shraddha boasts a clutch 62% win rate in three-game matches (13/21), an extraordinary 87% match win rate across 94 matches, a biggest win margin of 41 points.

With 21 different doubles partners over the years, Shraddha adapts to anyone:
  • Lexuan Wen (current primary partner) — 5 events together this season alone with 4 titles, building serious momentum (6 total events in GD, spanning 3 seasons)
  • Srinivas Raghav Subash (current partner) — 2 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Adhyaan Balaji (current partner) — 1 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Radhika Birur (current partner) — 1 events, 1 title together in GD

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 9 podium finishes across 4 tournaments. Shraddha is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Shraddha has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Shraddha has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Shraddha has been seeded #1 or #2 in 6 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 21 titles, Shraddha is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

  • Aayush Jaladurgam (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (3 events, best: #4)
  • Yuki Guan (current partner) — currently teaming up in GD (2 events, best: #5)

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 6 podium finishes across 5 tournaments. Siddhi is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

A Pennsylvania competitor, Sitar Nainish Sanghani has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-2), currently competing at the U13 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Sitar's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

Notably, Sitar has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Sitar is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #2, a doubles best of #4. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (6 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries) • ORC top-2 finisher.

With 8 different doubles partners over the years, Sitar adapts to anyone:
  • Zisheng (GaoGao) Fan (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in U13/XD (8 events, best: #5, spanning 3 seasons)
  • Haziqa H Mathani (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in GD/U13 (3 events, across 2 seasons)

Sitar has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Sitar has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Sitar brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

Representing Georgia, Suryansh Gupta is writing the opening pages of a badminton story, currently competing at the U15 level, with 3 entries across 1 tournaments. The willingness to compete is where every great player starts.

A true triple-threat, Suryansh competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

suryansh_sharma.name: Suryansh Sharma
suryansh_sharma.summary:
Representing New York, Suryansh Sharma is just getting started on the junior circuit with 2 tournament entries. Every champion's story has a beginning, and this is Suryansh's.

Suryansh competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

suyao_lee.name: Suyao Lee
suyao_lee.summary:
Representing North Carolina, Suyao Lee has shown real competitive teeth, currently competing at the U17 level, breaking into the semifinals 1 time. Across 4 entries in 3 tournaments, every deep run is a signal of growing strength.

Suyao competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

Suyao has competed at both U15 and U17, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

swanika_liang.name: Swanika Liang
swanika_liang.summary:
Representing California, Swanika Liang is writing the opening pages of a badminton story, currently competing at the U15 level, with 7 entries across 3 tournaments. The willingness to compete is where every great player starts.

Swanika is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

Swanika has competed at both U13 and U15, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

sydney_li.name: Sydney Li
sydney_li.summary:
Representing New Jersey, Sydney Li is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-2 finish at Selection places Sydney in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Sydney is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (14 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, Sydney competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 3 singles titles, 5 doubles titles, 5 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-2 (U17 GS, U15 GS, U19 GD, U15 GD) • Junior Nationals champion (GD U13) • 10 ORC titles.

Sydney has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 26 different players across the career:
  • Jun Heng Lai (current partner) — 2 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Rocky Yang — 6 events, 1 title together in U19/XD, across 2 seasons
  • Micah Cruz — 2 events, 1 title together in GD/U19

A veteran of the junior circuit, Sydney has competed across U13, U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Sydney has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Sydney has been seeded #1 or #2 in 9 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

With 6 seasons of competitive experience and 13 titles, Sydney is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

sydney_stachovic.name: Sydney Stachovic
sydney_stachovic.summary:
Representing Illinois, Sydney Stachovic keeps punching through to the quarterfinals, doing so 3 times, currently competing at the U19 level, building a strong foundation across 6 tournaments. Deeper runs are on the horizon.

What makes Sydney especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

Sydney has built chemistry with several doubles partners:
  • Rishi Jagannath — a trusted XD partner (4 events, best: #5, across 2 seasons)

Sydney has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Sydney brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

sylvia_yuqing_mei.name: Sylvia Yuqing Mei
sylvia_yuqing_mei.summary:
Representing Washington, Sylvia Yuqing Mei has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-3), currently competing at the U19 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Sylvia's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

Notably, Sylvia has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Sylvia competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 singles title, a doubles best of #2, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: ORC top-3 finisher.

With 5 different doubles partners over the years, Sylvia adapts to anyone:
  • Daniel Seungchan Park — 4 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Hansika - Malladi — 3 events in GD, best: #2

Sylvia has been on this journey through U13, U15, U19 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 2 titles, Sylvia is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

sze_kei_wong.name: Sze Kei Wong
sze_kei_wong.summary:
A New Jersey competitor, Sze Kei Wong broke through with a tournament title, currently competing at the U13 level, a moment that separates contenders from champions. With 6 entries across 2 tournaments, the competitive foundation is built — and more titles are within reach.

Notably, Sze has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Sze especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Sze has built chemistry with several doubles partners:
  • Sriaa Senthilkumar (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events, best: #5)
  • Ethan Qiu (current partner) — 1 events, 1 title together in BD

Sze has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.
